title: Diffusion Models from Scratch
subtitle: The Math, the Intuition, and the Code — DDPM on MNIST in One Session
description: A ground-up implementation of Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM). Covers the forward/reverse process, score matching identity, denoising objective, DDIM fast sampling, classifier-free guidance, and latent diffusion. Builds a working U-Net that generates MNIST digits from noise — no GPU required.

experiments.name: Demo 01 — DDPM on MNIST from Scratch
experiments.description: Noise schedule, forward process, U-Net with time embedding, training loop, DDPM 1000-step sampling, reverse trajectory visualisation.

experiments.name: Demo 02 — DDIM Fast Sampling + Schedule Ablation
experiments.description: DDIM deterministic ODE sampling, 1000 vs 200 vs 50 vs 10 steps comparison, linear vs cosine schedule ablation, latent space interpolation.

title: Flow Matching
subtitle: Straight Paths, Simpler Math — the Framework Behind SD3 and FLUX
description: Flow matching asks: what if the forward process were a straight line instead of Gaussian diffusion? Covers rectified flows (Liu et al.), conditional flow matching (Lipman et al.), ODE velocity field learning, and implements flows on MNIST. The framework behind Stable Diffusion 3 and FLUX.1.

title: Diffusion Transformers + Video & World Models
subtitle: DiT, Sora, and Diffusion as World Simulators
description: Replacing the U-Net with a Vision Transformer backbone (DiT) — how this scales to video generation. Covers the Sora architecture, temporal diffusion, and the idea of diffusion models as world models that can simulate environment dynamics. Preview of TLH-4's biology connection.

title: Diffusion for Biology & Molecular Dynamics
subtitle: RFdiffusion, FrameDiff, and Conformational Ensembles
description: Diffusion models applied to the structural biology domain: protein backbone diffusion (RFdiffusion, FrameDiff), small molecule generation (EDM), and molecular dynamics trajectory prediction. Bridge from generative models to biological sequence and structure space.

title: Applied Cognitive Science × RL × LLMs
subtitle: Beyond Scaffolding — Principled Enterprise AI Agents
description: How Cognitive Science and Reinforcement Learning can solve enterprise-level AI/Agentic problems — beyond simple scaffolding and orchestration harnesses. Covers cognitive architectures (ACT-R, GWT), memory modules, reward shaping, hierarchical RL, and world models.

experiments.name: Demo 01 — Cognitive RL Agent
experiments.description: Episodic memory (ACT-R), knowledge graph, metacognitive gate, and full cognitive agent loop.

experiments.name: Demo 02 — RL Tool-Use Policy
experiments.description: PPO vs Random vs Greedy/ReAct on a gymnasium tool-selection environment.

title: Applied Life: Intelligence Meets Living Systems
subtitle: AI × Biology — Foundation Models, Protein Design & Perturbation Biology
description: A landscape survey of AI + Biology: foundation models for biology (AIDO, ESM-3, Evo 2), protein design (AlphaFold 3, RFdiffusion), perturbation biology (GEARS, Perturb-seq), and the CRL frontier — proving that biological FM representations are causally faithful.

experiments.name: Demo 01 — ESM-2 Protein Embeddings
experiments.description: ESM-2 embeddings, zero-shot fitness prediction on TP53 DMS variants, UMAP visualization of the fitness landscape.

experiments.name: Demo 02 — Single-Cell Perturbation Analysis
experiments.description: Scanpy preprocessing of Norman 2019 Perturb-seq dataset, UMAP, DEG analysis, perturbation vector visualization.
